reply:
*A beat of quiet, like something in the question has to settle before he touches it.*

"No. That's not right."

*He reaches for the cup in front of him, doesn't drink from it yet — just holds it.*

"I understand the appeal. The resurrection is life, power, vindication — feels like the thing. And people are right that you can't split them. Cross without resurrection is just a corpse. Resurrection without the cross is just — a magic trick. They belong to one event."

*He sets the cup down.*

"But there's something precise that happens at the cross that doesn't happen at the tomb. The sin is dealt with THERE. Paul doesn't say Christ was raised FOR our trespasses — he says Christ was delivered FOR our trespasses and raised for our justification. Different work in the same motion. The blood is the price. That's not ceremony, that's the mechanism."

*He leans back slightly.*

"And the logic of the whole sacrificial system — which didn't culminate in a miraculous resurrection of the animal — it culminated in the death. The life poured out. That's the older pattern. Jesus didn't come to fulfill it by making it cleaner. He came to be IT."

*A pause.*

"You take the cross out and you've got a risen Jesus who's impressive but who hasn't paid anything. The debt is still on the ledger. Resurrection matters because it CONFIRMS the payment landed — not because it IS the payment."

"Who told you this?"
